title: Dos-à-dos double pamphlet
subtitle: Two booklets sharing a back-to-back cover board
excerpt: A dos-à-dos binding holds two separate pamphlets back-to-back, each opening in the opposite direction from the other. The centre board serves as the back cover of both. The structure suits paired notebooks, a bilingual text, or a notes-and-sketches combination where the reader wants two distinct sections in one object.
sourceNotes: Dos-à-dos binding documented in Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901), notes on unusual structures. The form appears in sixteenth- and seventeenth-century European devotional books, where two texts (prayer and psalm, Old and New Testament excerpt) were bound back-to-back for portability. Cyril Davenport, The Book: Its History and Development (1907), chapter on curiosities of binding structure.

glossaryTerms.definition: French for 'back-to-back'. A binding in which two pamphlets share a common centre board and open in opposite directions, the spine of one becoming the foredge of the other.
A group of folded sheets sewn together at the fold to form one section of a text block.
The outer edge of a book's pages, opposite the spine.

content.text: houses two pamphlets in one object. The reader opens one from the left in the conventional way; to read the second, the reader flips the book over and opens it from what was the foredge of the first. The three boards that make up the structure, front of book A, the shared centre board, front of book B, are covered with cloth and connected at each spine by the pamphlet sewing of the respective text block.

attrs.title: Cutting tools and adhesives
attrs.body: Cut boards in two or three passes; PVA dries quickly, so apply to one board at a time on scrap paper and press immediately.

items.name: Cartridge paper, 90 gsm, long-grain A6
items.qty: 16 sheets (two pamphlets of 4 sheets each, 8 pages per pamphlet)

items.name: Bookbinding board, 1.5 mm, cut to A6 + 3 mm squares on three sides
items.qty: 3 boards (two outer covers and one shared centre)

items.name: Book cloth for covering boards
items.qty: One long piece or three separate pieces, 15 mm turn-in on all sides

items.name: Waxed linen bookbinding thread, 4-cord
items.qty: Approximately 0.8 m per pamphlet (1.6 m total)

items.name: Beeswax block, bone folder, acid-free PVA
Craft knife, metal ruler, cutting mat

content.text: Cut three boards to A6 + 3 mm on the three open edges: for an A6 text block (148 × 105 mm), each board is 151 × 108 mm. The centre board serves as both back covers, so it receives cloth on both faces. Cover the two outer boards on the outside face only, with a 15 mm turn-in. Cover the centre board on both faces, with 15 mm turn-in on all edges, working one face at a time with a drying period in between.

, one for each pamphlet. Fold and score each signature with the bone folder. Mark three sewing stations on the fold of each: one at the centre, one 8 mm from the head, one 8 mm from the tail. Pierce all three stations with the awl from inside to outside.

Sew pamphlet A first. Position the outer cover A face-down on the table, then lay signature A open on top. Enter the awl through the outer cover at each station, using the signature holes as guides. Thread the needle, wax the thread, and sew pamphlet A: enter through the outside cover at the centre station, through to the inside of the signature; out at the head station; back in at the tail station; back out at the centre. Tie off with two half-hitches at the centre, with both tails inside the signature.

Sew pamphlet B in the same way, but sew through the centre board (which is the back cover of both pamphlets) rather than an outer cover. Pierce the centre board at the same three station positions. Sew signature B to the centre board. The sewing is identical to pamphlet A.

Lay pamphlet A sewn-to-outer-cover-A on the table. Position the centre board at the spine edge of pamphlet A, with pamphlet A's text block between them. Align the board edges. Now position pamphlet B with its text block opening in the opposite direction: its spine is at the
of pamphlet A. The centre board, which has pamphlet B sewn to it, is already in position as the back cover of pamphlet B. Place outer cover B at the far edge to complete the structure.
Press the assembled dos-à-dos under weights for thirty minutes. The structure does not require additional adhesive: the sewing holds both pamphlets securely to the three boards. Store flat.

items.symptom: The two pamphlets do not align at the edges when closed
items.cause: The text blocks were not cut to exactly the same dimensions, or the centre board is off-centre relative to both pamphlet spines.
items.fix: Measure both text blocks before sewing. The spine edge of each must meet the corresponding spine edge of the centre board squarely. Trim the longer block's foredge after sewing if needed.
